subject: An insectoid humanoid assassin — the Kraul — perched in a crouched ambush pose, dropping or descending from above with a barbed stinger-tail arcing overhead. Chitinous limbs, mandibled head, hunched silhouette.
description: A chitinous insect-person — the Kraul — is captured mid-descent against the side of a stone wall or pillar, body hunched and angular, with a long segmented tail curving up behind it tipped in a wicked barb. The limbs are thin and many-jointed, ending in clawed grip-points. The head is small and mandibled, oriented down toward unseen prey. The palette is overwhelmingly muted green and ochre — moss, sap, and rot — with darker shadow pooling under the figure. Background is a vague stone surface receding into murky greenish gloom, giving an impression of an underground or twilit street ambush. The composition is tight and vertical, emphasizing the creature's stalking, predatory geometry.
foreground: kraul insect-assassin clinging to a vertical surface, tail-stinger raised
background: vague stone wall / pillar fading into greenish gloom
emotion: predatory focus, ambush stillness, lethal patience
